# Page 1^page=1
Name: Lisa A English | DOB: 7/25/1964 | MRN: 055656 | PCP: Jung Wook Kang Smith, MD | Legal Name: Lisa A English Progress Notes Signed Jun 22, 2026 Deaconess Care Group Hospital Progress Note Patient Name: Lisa A English MRN: 055656 DOB: 7/25/1964 Date of Admission: 6/19/2026 Date of Service: 6/22/2026 PMD: Kang Smith, Jung Wook Chief complaint: Debility History The patient is a 61 y.o. female with a past medical history notable for chronic debility/wheelchair-bound, former smoker/severe COPD oxygen dependent on chronic steroids and dupixent, chronic respiratory failure with hypoxia and hypercapnia, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, type 2 diabetes mellitus, CAD, depression/anxiety/PTSD, chronic anemia, kidney stones, GERD. She was discharged home yesterday from hospital due to severe COPD exacerbation, short words Augmentin for possible right upper extremity cellulitis. She presented back from home via EMS due to an inability to care for herself, she requires help at home in transition between recliner to the scooter, her son is not able to help her, she has been sitting in the recliner since discharge yesterday from hospital. Afebrile, heart rate 180, respiratory 20, BP 152/69, 97% on 2 L Lab significant for WBC 11.5, hemoglobin 9.3, glucose 109, CPK 22, ABG pH 7.4/62.8 She received DuoNeb, admission requested for potential placement. Medically ready. Pending placement Consultants: None Progress Notes by Ahmad Malik at 06/22/26 1406

# Page 2^page=2
Procedures: None Anti-infectives: None Subjective: Patient was seen and evaluated at the bedside. Denies new concerns. Reporting anxiety requesting IV morphine Objective: Physical Exam: General: Awake, alert and oriented, anxious Cardiac: S1-S2 regular rate and rhythm with no murmurs, gallops or rubs Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally with no wheezes, rhonchi or crackles Abdomen: Soft, nontender, nondistended with no organomegaly Extremities: No edema Neuro: No focal deficits Psych: Affect normal, speech fluid and normal Labs Reviewed Vitals: 06/22/26 0803 06/22/26 0900 06/22/26 1030 06/22/26 1031 BP: Pulse: 80 Resp: Temp: TempSrc: SpO2: 94% 95% 95% Weight: Height: Recent Results (from the past 24 hours) POCT GLUCOSE Collection Time: 06/21/26 5:54 PM Result Value Ref Range POC Glucose 181 (H) 70 - 99 MG/DL POCT GLUCOSE Collection Time: 06/21/26 9:38 PM Result Value Ref Range POC Glucose 150 (H) 70 - 99 MG/DL POCT GLUCOSE Collection Time: 06/22/26 9:36 AM Result Value Ref Range POC Glucose 170 (H) 70 - 99 MG/DL

# Page 3^page=3
Medications Reviewed: Assessment/Plan: Morbid obesity due to excess calories (HCC) Physical debility DM hyperosmolarity type II, uncontrolled (HCC) Chronic respiratory failure with hypoxia and hypercapnia Generalized anxiety disorder Dependence on non-invasive ventilation COPD, end-stage Plan: PRN nebs q4h, p.o. prednisone at home dose Mucinex, Tessalon p.r.n. for cough Hold metformin, ISS Home O2 2-3 L nasal can baseline, BiPAP q.h.s. PT/OT, fall precautions Consult case management for potential placement DVT prophylaxis: Lovenox, SCD Code status: Full code • aspirin 162 mg Oral Daily • budesonide 500 mcg Nebulization BID • busPIRone 5 mg Oral BID • docusate 100 mg Oral BID • enoxaparin 40 mg Subcutaneous Q24H • FLUoxetine 20 mg Oral Daily • furosemide 20 mg Oral Daily • guaiFENesin 400 mg Oral qid (q4h while awake) • insulin lispro (HumaLOG, AdmeLOG) vial 0-6 Units Subcutaneous TID AC • ipratropium-albuterol 3 mL Nebulization 6 times per day • lisinopril 20 mg Oral Daily • metoprolol succinate 50 mg Oral BID • pantoprazole 40 mg Oral Daily • predniSONE 20 mg Oral Daily • sulfamethoxazole- trimethoprim 1 tablet Oral Once per day on Monday Wednesday Friday • theophylline 300 mg Oral Daily

# Page 4^page=4

Please note that these notes will include professional medical terminology that may be somewhat confusing without some interpretation from your medical professional team. The intent of progress notes is to communicate information between any medical professionals involved in your case, or to serve as future reference for myself or any other provider when reviewing your case, as well as a reference for the patient viewing the record. Please ask a member of the medical team if you have any questions about terminology or content of note. This has been electronically signed by: _______________________________ Ahmad T Malik, MD Deaconess Care Group Hospitalist 6/22/2026
